parallel--e4278597-a92d-45a0-b28a-9267bf.name: Implement and review Windows Event Logs for relevant indicators

description: This is an automatically generated playbook based on the question: A detections playbook for T1040 of MITRE ATT&CK Matrix for Enterprise framework is required.The target platform is Windows. You can find more information at https://attack.mitre.org/detectionstrategies/DET0314#AN0875 . The description of the approach follows: Detects suspicious execution of network monitoring tools (e.g., Wireshark, tshark, Microsoft Message Analyzer), driver loading indicative of promiscuous mode, or non-admin user privilege escalation to access NICs for capture.

items.content: Monitor process creation events for known network monitoring tools (e.g., Wireshark, tshark, Microsoft Message Analyzer, tcpdump, WinDump).
Inspect command-line arguments of network monitoring processes for suspicious flags (e.g., promiscuous mode, output redirection, interface selection).
Monitor for driver loads associated with network monitoring or promiscuous mode (e.g., npf.sys for WinPcap/Npcap).
Detect attempts to enable promiscuous mode on network interfaces via system calls or PowerShell commands.
Audit for privilege escalation events where non-admin users gain elevated permissions to access network interfaces or install drivers.
Correlate network monitoring tool execution with unusual network traffic patterns or data exfiltration attempts.
Security event ID 4688: Monitor process creation with detailed command-line logging enabled
System event ID 6: Driver loaded events
Security event ID 4673: Privilege escalation or sensitive privilege use
Implement and review Windows Event Logs for relevant indicators
Deploy and tune EDR or SIEM rules to alert on suspicious combinations of the above activities.
Establish baselines of normal administrative use of network tools to reduce false positives.
